briefdescription.para: binary table lookup for user-supplied name

simplesect.para: pointer to range function if found, otherwise NULL

briefdescription.para: Check whether the character is part of the UCS Block.

simplesect.para: 1 if true, 0 if false and -1 on unknown block

briefdescription.para: [1] regExp ::= branch ( '|' branch )*
Free a regexp state.
Free a regexp atom.
Checks if both strings are equal or have the same content.

detaileddescription.para: "*" can be used as a wildcard in valStr; "|" is used as a separator of substrings in both expStr and valStr.

simplesect.para: 1 if the comparison is satisfied and the number of substrings is equal, 0 otherwise.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlRegAtomType type, int codepoint, int neg, int start, int end, const xmlChar *blockName)
memberdef.name: xmlRegCheckCharacterRange

briefdescription.para: Handle an out of memory condition.
Handle a compilation failure.
Check whether the associated regexp is determinist, should be called after xmlFAEliminateEpsilonTransitions.
Allocate a two-dimensional array and set all elements to zero.

simplesect.para: the new array or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Allocate a new regexp and fill it with the result from the parser.

parameterdescription.para: the parser context used to build it

simplesect.para: the new regexp or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Allocate a new regexp parser context.

simplesect.para: the new context or NULL in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlRegParserCtxtPtr ctxt, int neg, xmlRegAtomType type, int start, int end)
memberdef.name: xmlRegNewRange

briefdescription.para: Allocate a new regexp range.

simplesect.para: the new range or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Free a regexp range.
Copy a regexp range.

simplesect.para: the new copy or NULL in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Allocate a new atom.

simplesect.para: the new atom or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Allocate a new regexp range.

simplesect.para: the new atom or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Free a regexp parser context.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlRegParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlRegAtomPtr atom, int neg, xmlRegAtomType type, int start, int end, xmlChar *blockName)
memberdef.name: xmlRegAtomAddRange

memberdef.name: xmlRegStateAddTrans

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlRegParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlRegStatePtr from, xmlRegStatePtr to, int lax)
memberdef.name: xmlFAGenerateAllTransition

parameterdescription.para: the target state or NULL for building a new one
the target state or NULL for building a new one

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlRegParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlRegStatePtr from, xmlRegStatePtr to, int counter)
memberdef.name: xmlFAGenerateCountedEpsilonTransition

parameterdescription.para: the target state or NULL for building a new one

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlRegParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlRegStatePtr from, xmlRegStatePtr to, int counter)
memberdef.name: xmlFAGenerateCountedTransition

parameterdescription.para: the target state or NULL for building a new one

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlRegParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlRegStatePtr from, xmlRegStatePtr to, xmlRegAtomPtr atom)
memberdef.name: xmlFAGenerateTransitions

parameterdescription.para: the target state or NULL for building a new one

simplesect.para: 0 if success and -1 in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlRegParserCtxtPtr ctxt, int fromnr, int tonr, int counter)
memberdef.name: xmlFAReduceEpsilonTransitions

parameterdescription.para: should that transition be associated to a counted

briefdescription.para: Eliminating general epsilon transitions can get costly in the general algorithm due to the large amount of generated new transitions and associated comparisons.

detaileddescription.para:
However for simple epsilon transition used just to separate building blocks when generating the automata this can be reduced to state elimination:
if there exists an epsilon from X to Y
if there is no other transition from X then X and Y are semantically equivalent and X can be eliminated If X is the start state then make Y the start state, else replace the target of all transitions to X by transitions to Y.

If X is a final state, skip it. Otherwise it would be necessary to manipulate counters for this case when eliminating state 2: State 1 has a transition with an atom to state 2. State 2 is final and has an epsilon transition to state 1.

briefdescription.para: Compares two atoms type to check whether they intersect in some ways, this is used by xmlFACompareAtoms only.

simplesect.para: 1 if they may intersect and 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Compares two atoms to check whether they are the same exactly this is used to remove equivalent transitions.

parameterdescription.para: if not set only compare string pointers

simplesect.para: 1 if same and 0 otherwise

briefdescription.para: Compares two atoms to check whether they intersect in some ways, this is used by xmlFAComputesDeterminism and xmlFARecurseDeterminism only.

parameterdescription.para: if not set only compare string pointers

simplesect.para: 1 if yes and 0 otherwise

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlRegParserCtxtPtr ctxt, xmlRegStatePtr state, int fromnr, int tonr, xmlRegAtomPtr atom)
memberdef.name: xmlFARecurseDeterminism

briefdescription.para: Check whether the associated regexp is determinist, should be called after xmlFAEliminateEpsilonTransitions.
Reset flags after checking determinism.
Build a context used for progressive evaluation of a regexp.

parameterdescription.para: a callback function used for handling progresses in the automata matching phase
the context data associated to the callback in this context

simplesect.para: the new context

briefdescription.para: Free the structures associated to a regular expression evaluation context.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlRegExecCtxtPtr exec, xmlRegexpPtr comp, const xmlChar *value, void *data)
memberdef.name: xmlRegCompactPushString

briefdescription.para: Push one input token in the execution context.

parameterdescription.para: the precompiled exec with a compact table
data associated to the token to reuse in callbacks

simplesect.para: 1 if the regexp reached a final state, 0 if non-final, and a negative value in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlRegExecCtxtPtr exec, const xmlChar *value, void *data, int compound)
memberdef.name: xmlRegExecPushStringInternal

briefdescription.para: Push one input token in the execution context.

parameterdescription.para: a regexp execution context or NULL to indicate the end
data associated to the token to reuse in callbacks

simplesect.para: 1 if the regexp reached a final state, 0 if non-final, and a negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Push one input token in the execution context.

parameterdescription.para: a regexp execution context or NULL to indicate the end
data associated to the token to reuse in callbacks

simplesect.para: 1 if the regexp reached a final state, 0 if non-final, and a negative value in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Push one input token in the execution context.

parameterdescription.para: a regexp execution context or NULL to indicate the end
data associated to the token to reuse in callbacks

simplesect.para: 1 if the regexp reached a final state, 0 if non-final, and a negative value in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlRegExecCtxtPtr exec, int err, int *nbval, int *nbneg, xmlChar **values, int *terminal)
memberdef.name: xmlRegExecGetValues

briefdescription.para: Extract information from the regexp execution.

detaileddescription.para: Internal routine to implement xmlRegExecNextValues and xmlRegExecErrInfo

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the number of accepted values IN/OUT
pointer to the array of acceptable values
return value if this was a terminal state

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Extract information from the regexp execution.

detaileddescription.para: The parameter values must point to an array of nbval string pointers on return nbval will contain the number of possible strings in that state and the values array will be updated with them. The string values returned will be freed with the exec context and don't need to be deallocated.

parameterdescription.para: pointer to the number of accepted values IN/OUT
pointer to the array of acceptable values
return value if this was a terminal state

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or -1 in case of error.

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlRegExecCtxt *exec, const xmlChar **string, int *nbval, int *nbneg, xmlChar **values, int *terminal)
memberdef.name: xmlRegExecErrInfo

briefdescription.para: Extract error information from the regexp execution.

detaileddescription.para: The parameter string will be updated with the value pushed and not accepted, the parameter values must point to an array of nbval string pointers on return nbval will contain the number of possible strings in that state and the values array will be updated with them. The string values returned will be freed with the exec context and don't need to be deallocated.

parameterdescription.para: a regexp execution context generating an error
return value for the error string
pointer to the number of accepted values IN/OUT
pointer to the array of acceptable values
return value if this was a terminal state

simplesect.para: 0 in case of success or -1 in case of error.

briefdescription.para: Clear errors in the context, allowing to recover from errors on specific scenarios.

simplesect.para: it doesn's reset the last internal libxml2 error

briefdescription.para: [27] charProp ::= IsCategory | IsBlock [28] IsCategory ::= Letters | Marks | Numbers | Punctuation | Separators | Symbols | Others [29] Letters ::= 'L' [ultmo]? [30] Marks ::= 'M' [nce]? [31] Numbers ::= 'N' [dlo]? [32] Punctuation ::= 'P' [cdseifo]? [33] Separators ::= 'Z' [slp]? [34] Symbols ::= 'S' [mcko]? [35] Others ::= 'C' [cfon]? [36] IsBlock ::= 'Is' [a-zA-Z0-9#x2D]+
[14] posCharGroup ::= ( charRange | charClassEsc )+

[13] charGroup ::= posCharGroup | negCharGroup | charClassSub [15] negCharGroup ::= '^' posCharGroup [16] charClassSub ::= ( posCharGroup | negCharGroup ) '-' charClassExpr [12] charClassExpr ::= '[' charGroup ']'
[11] charClass ::= charClassEsc | charClassExpr [12] charClassExpr ::= '[' charGroup ']'
[8] QuantExact ::= [0-9]+

simplesect.para: 0 if success or -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: [4] quantifier ::= [?*+] | ( '{' quantity '}' ) [5] quantity ::= quantRange | quantMin | QuantExact [6] quantRange ::= QuantExact ',' QuantExact [7] quantMin ::= QuantExact ',' [8] QuantExact ::= [0-9]+
[9] atom ::= Char | charClass | ( '(' regExp ')' )
[3] piece ::= atom quantifier?
to is used to optimize by removing duplicate path in automata in expressions like (a|b)(c|d)

parameterdescription.para: optional target to the end of the branch

briefdescription.para: No-op since 2.14.0.

parameterdescription.para: the file for the output debug

briefdescription.para: Parses an XML Schemas regular expression.

detaileddescription.para: Parses a regular expression conforming to XML Schemas Part 2 Datatype Appendix F and builds an automata suitable for testing strings against that regular expression.

simplesect.para: the compiled expression or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Check if the regular expression matches a string.

parameterdescription.para: the value to check against the regular expression

simplesect.para: 1 if it matches, 0 if not and a negative value in case of error

briefdescription.para: Check if the regular expression is deterministic.

detaileddescription.para: DTD and XML Schemas require a deterministic content model, so the automaton compiled from the regex must be a DFA.
The runtime of this function is quadratic in the number of outgoing edges, causing serious worst-case performance issues.

simplesect.para: 1 if it yes, 0 if not and a negative value in case of error

briefdescription.para: Free a regexp.
Create a new automata.

simplesect.para: the new object or NULL in case of failure

briefdescription.para: Free an automata.
Set some flags on the automata.
Initial state lookup.

simplesect.para: the initial state of the automata

briefdescription.para: Makes that state a final state.

simplesect.para: 0 or -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Add a transition.

detaileddescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds a transition from the from state to the target state activated by the value of token

parameterdescription.para: the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
the input string associated to that transition
data passed to the callback function if the transition is activated

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlAutomata *am, xmlAutomataState *from, xmlAutomataState *to, const xmlChar *token, const xmlChar *token2, void *data)
memberdef.name: xmlAutomataNewTransition2

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds a transition from the from state to the target state activated by the value of token

parameterdescription.para: the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
the first input string associated to that transition
the second input string associated to that transition
data passed to the callback function if the transition is activated

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlAutomata *am, xmlAutomataState *from, xmlAutomataState *to, const xmlChar *token, const xmlChar *token2, void *data)
memberdef.name: xmlAutomataNewNegTrans

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds a transition from the from state to the target state activated by any value except (token,token2) Note that if token2 is not NULL, then (X, NULL) won't match to follow the semantic of XSD ##other.

parameterdescription.para: the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
the first input string associated to that transition
the second input string associated to that transition
data passed to the callback function if the transition is activated

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlAutomata *am, xmlAutomataState *from, xmlAutomataState *to, const xmlChar *token, const xmlChar *token2, int min, int max, void *data)
memberdef.name: xmlAutomataNewCountTrans2

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds a transition from the from state to the target state activated by a succession of input of value token and token2 and whose number is between min and max

parameterdescription.para: the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
the input string associated to that transition
the second input string associated to that transition
the minimum successive occurrences of token
the maximum successive occurrences of token

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlAutomata *am, xmlAutomataState *from, xmlAutomataState *to, const xmlChar *token, int min, int max, void *data)
memberdef.name: xmlAutomataNewCountTrans

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds a transition from the from state to the target state activated by a succession of input of value token and whose number is between min and max

parameterdescription.para: the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
the input string associated to that transition
the minimum successive occurrences of token
the maximum successive occurrences of token

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlAutomata *am, xmlAutomataState *from, xmlAutomataState *to, const xmlChar *token, const xmlChar *token2, int min, int max, void *data)
memberdef.name: xmlAutomataNewOnceTrans2

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds a transition from the from state to the target state activated by a succession of input of value token and token2 and whose number is between min and max, moreover that transition can only be crossed once.

parameterdescription.para: the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
the input string associated to that transition
the second input string associated to that transition
the minimum successive occurrences of token
the maximum successive occurrences of token

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlAutomata *am, xmlAutomataState *from, xmlAutomataState *to, const xmlChar *token, int min, int max, void *data)
memberdef.name: xmlAutomataNewOnceTrans

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds a transition from the from state to the target state activated by a succession of input of value token and whose number is between min and max, moreover that transition can only be crossed once.

parameterdescription.para: the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
the input string associated to that transition
the minimum successive occurrences of token
the maximum successive occurrences of token

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Create a new disconnected state in the automata.

simplesect.para: the new state or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds an epsilon transition from the from state to the target state.

parameterdescription.para: the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds a an ALL transition from the from state to the target state.

detaileddescription.para: That transition is an epsilon transition allowed only when all transitions from the from node have been activated.

parameterdescription.para: the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
allow to transition if not all all transitions have been activated

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Create a new counter.

parameterdescription.para: the minimal value on the counter
the maximal value on the counter

simplesect.para: the counter number or -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds an epsilon transition from the from state to the target state which will increment the counter provided.

parameterdescription.para: the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
the counter associated to that transition

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds an epsilon transition from the from state to the target state which will be allowed only if the counter is within the right range.

parameterdescription.para: the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
the counter associated to that transition

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Compile the automata into a Reg Exp ready for being executed.

detaileddescription.para: The automata should be free after this point.

simplesect.para: the compiled regexp or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Checks if an automata is determinist.

simplesect.para: 1 if true, 0 if not, and -1 in case of error
